key_insight: INCARBench shows that LLMs can often produce semantically plausible VASP INCAR settings, but scientifically valid configuration still breaks down when parameters are tightly coupled.
engineering_relevance: This benchmark highlights that AI tools for computational materials engineering need constraint-aware validation, not just text-level or parameter-level correctness.
method: Built INCARBench to test LLMs on VASP input configuration.
Evaluated both configuration generation and repair tasks.
Scored models on semantic accuracy, policy accuracy, and task-critical correctness.
evidence_signal: Across 19 model configurations, the study found strong gaps between semantic/policy accuracy and true task-critical correctness, especially for DFT+$U$, magnetism, and correlated-material settings.
watch_item: High-quality repair is not the same as safe preservation: models may fix wrong parameters while unintentionally changing already-correct ones.
paper_title: INCARBench: A Benchmark for Scientific Configuration in VASP INCAR by Large Language Models
